family_title: Check for peanuts directly
family_summary: Peanut risk needs a fast ingredient question of its own because the free allergy baseline is broader than the real moment at the table.
english_text: Does this have peanuts?
context: Use this before ordering or eating when peanuts are the specific ingredient that matters.
search_aliases: does this have peanuts|has peanuts|peanut check|peanuts
notes: Phase 1B premium food scaffold, not live yet

family_title: Ask for no meat
family_summary: This is the short correction family for dishes that look safe at first but need a clear no-meat follow-up.
english_text: No meat, please.
context: Use this when you need the kitchen or vendor to remove meat instead of guessing from the menu.
search_aliases: no meat|without meat|vegetarian no meat
notes: Phase 1B premium food scaffold, not live yet

family_title: Remove a specific ingredient
family_summary: Point-and-remove phrasing is more believable premium value than generic dietary theory because it fixes the actual plate in front of you.
english_text: Can you make it without this ingredient?
context: Use this while pointing at the ingredient you need removed from the dish.
search_aliases: without this ingredient|leave this out|remove this ingredient
notes: Phase 1B premium food scaffold, not live yet

family_title: Fix a dish that is too spicy
family_summary: Travelers often ask for mild food first, but premium should cover the recovery line when the dish still arrives too spicy.
english_text: This is too spicy for me.
context: Use this after the food arrives when the heat level is higher than you can handle.
search_aliases: too spicy|this is too spicy|not mild enough|too hot
notes: Phase 1B premium food scaffold, not live yet

family_title: Ask which dish is safer
family_summary: When the menu is unclear, premium should help the traveler choose the safer option instead of gambling on the first guess.
english_text: Which dish is safest for this allergy?
context: Use this while pointing at your allergy card or ingredient note when you need the best option, not just a yes-or-no.
search_aliases: which dish is safe|safe dish for allergy|which one is safest
notes: Phase 1B premium food scaffold, not live yet

family_title: Flag that the dish still has meat
family_summary: This is the correction family for the moment when the order comes out with meat after you already tried to avoid it.
english_text: This has meat in it.
context: Use this when the dish arrives with meat even though you expected it without.
search_aliases: has meat in it|there is meat in this|this still has meat
notes: Phase 1B premium food scaffold, not live yet

family_title: Say the verification code is not arriving
family_summary: OTP failure is a real travel blocker because it breaks rides, payments, check-ins, and logins all at once.
english_text: The verification code is not arriving.
context: Use this when you are waiting on an OTP or SMS code that still has not come through.
search_aliases: otp not arriving|code not arriving|verification code|sms code
notes: Phase 1B premium connectivity scaffold, not live yet

family_title: Say the Wi-Fi password is not working
family_summary: This is the follow-up after the free Wi-Fi password ask when the answer you got still does not connect.
english_text: The Wi-Fi password is not working.
context: Use this after you entered the password and still cannot get online.
search_aliases: wifi password not working|wrong wifi password|password does not work
notes: Phase 1B premium connectivity scaffold, not live yet

family_title: Say the Wi-Fi login page will not load
family_summary: Captive-portal failure is a distinct problem from the password itself and deserves its own recovery family.
english_text: The Wi-Fi login page is not loading.
context: Use this when the network appears, but the sign-in page never opens or keeps failing.
search_aliases: wifi login page|login page not loading|wifi sign in page
notes: Phase 1B premium connectivity scaffold, not live yet

family_title: Say there is no signal here
family_summary: Signal failure is one of the simplest but most important phone recovery families because the next step is often location or network help.
english_text: There is no signal here.
context: Use this when calls, texts, or data cannot connect because the signal is missing or too weak.
search_aliases: no signal here|no signal|weak signal|cannot get signal
notes: Phase 1B premium connectivity scaffold, not live yet

family_title: Say the data is not working
family_summary: This covers the painful middle state where the SIM exists but maps, rides, or payment apps still fail.
english_text: My data is not working.
context: Use this when the SIM or eSIM shows up, but mobile data still will not connect.
search_aliases: data not working|mobile data not working|no data
notes: Phase 1B premium connectivity scaffold, not live yet

family_title: Ask for help activating the SIM
family_summary: SIM purchase is the free baseline; activation help is the premium recovery step when setup gets stuck.
english_text: Can you help me activate the SIM?
context: Use this when you bought the SIM or eSIM but still need staff help to get it working.
search_aliases: activate sim|help activate sim|sim setup help|activate esim
notes: Phase 1B premium connectivity scaffold, not live yet

family_title: Ask someone to translate for you
family_summary: Human translation support is premium-value recovery because it rescues the interaction when your phrase alone is no longer enough.
english_text: Can you help translate for me?
context: Use this when the situation is getting important and you need another person to bridge the conversation.
search_aliases: help translate for me|can you translate|translation help
notes: Phase 1B premium help scaffold, not live yet

family_title: Ask someone to call a number for you
family_summary: Travelers often have the right number but not the language or confidence to make the call themselves.
english_text: Can you call this number for me?
context: Use this while showing a phone number for the hotel, airline, embassy, landlord, or another contact.
search_aliases: call this number for me|can you call for me|call this number
notes: Phase 1B premium help scaffold, not live yet

family_title: Ask for help contacting the embassy
family_summary: Embassy help becomes much more believable paid value when the ask is contact and escalation, not just naming the place.
english_text: Can you help me contact the embassy?
context: Use this when you need someone to call, message, or direct you to embassy or consulate help.
search_aliases: contact the embassy|help contact embassy|embassy help
notes: Phase 1B premium help scaffold, not live yet

family_title: Ask for a printed copy of a document
family_summary: Document recovery often depends on a printed copy, not just the file on your phone.
english_text: I need a printed copy of this document.
context: Use this while showing a phone screen or email when you need a paper copy for a report, check-in, or passport issue.
search_aliases: printed copy of this document|print this document|need a printout
notes: Phase 1B premium help scaffold, not live yet

family_title: Ask someone to come with you
family_summary: When a situation feels risky or confusing, asking someone to come with you can matter more than one extra phrase.
english_text: Can you come with me?
context: Use this when you need support going to the police desk, embassy, hospital, counter, or another stressful next stop.
search_aliases: come with me|can you come with me|go with me
notes: Phase 1B premium help scaffold, not live yet

family_title: Ask for the tourist police
family_summary: Tourist police is a more actionable escalation target than generic emergency language when the issue is theft, scam, or document trouble.
english_text: I need the tourist police.
context: Use this when you need police help that is used to dealing with visitors, reports, or theft.
search_aliases: tourist police|need tourist police|police for tourists
notes: Phase 1B premium emergency scaffold, not live yet

family_title: Ask to go to the police station
family_summary: This moves the situation from vague help into the exact official place where the next step can happen.
english_text: Please take me to the police station.
context: Use this when you need to make a report or get official help in person.
search_aliases: take me to the police station|go to police station|police station
notes: Phase 1B premium emergency scaffold, not live yet

family_title: Report a stolen phone
family_summary: Phone theft deserves its own family because it combines safety, identity, payments, and navigation risk immediately.
english_text: My phone was stolen.
context: Use this when the problem is theft, not just losing or leaving the phone behind.
search_aliases: my phone was stolen|phone stolen|stolen phone
notes: Phase 1B premium emergency scaffold, not live yet

family_title: Report a stolen wallet
family_summary: Wallet theft is different from a missing bag because it hits cards, cash, and ID all at once.
english_text: My wallet was stolen.
context: Use this when the missing item contains money, cards, or identification and theft is the problem.
search_aliases: wallet stolen|my wallet was stolen|stolen wallet
notes: Phase 1B premium emergency scaffold, not live yet

family_title: Ask for a passport-loss report
family_summary: Passport loss becomes real travel paralysis fast, so premium should cover the exact official document the traveler needs next.
english_text: I need a report for my lost passport.
context: Use this when you need the police report or official record required for embassy or replacement help.
search_aliases: report for lost passport|lost passport report|passport report
notes: Phase 1B premium emergency scaffold, not live yet

family_title: Ask to go somewhere safe now
family_summary: This is the direct safety recovery line for the moment when staying in place no longer feels acceptable.
english_text: Please take me somewhere safe.
context: Use this when you need to leave the current location immediately and get to a safer place.
search_aliases: take me somewhere safe|somewhere safe|help me get somewhere safe
